The Real Reason I Switched from $8,000 High-Ticket to $8/Month Skool Membership
Video Script — Screenshare Format
01 HOOK ~15 seconds
  • Everyone in this space is telling you what Skool pricing model worked for them — low-ticket, high-ticket, freemium, paid. Nobody is actually giving you a real framework to figure out what works for you.
  • I watched freemium fail the moment I started running ads. You bring people in for free and most of them never upgrade — because you can't just give everything away and expect people to ascend without a real conversion mechanism.
  • So if you're on Skool, thinking about it, not sure what to charge — or high-ticket just isn't hitting the way it used to — that's not a you problem.
  • I'm going to show you the actual math, the actual model I switched to, and why. And then let you decide what's right for you.
02 PROOF ~30 seconds
  • I've taken clients from no recurring revenue to 62 paid members in two weeks
  • From $3K months to $20K months
  • From zero sales to multiple four-figure clients in weeks
  • I know what works. I also know exactly what doesn't — and I learned that the expensive way.
03 PROMISE 1 sentence
  • By the end of this, you'll know in your gut which model is actually aligned for you — and you'll stop second-guessing yourself.
04 PATH Rapid-fire roadmap
  • We're covering:
    • Why high-ticket stopped feeling right — and what finally broke it for me
    • The real math behind $8/month (it's not what you think)
    • Why Skool specifically makes this model work
    • The one question to ask yourself before you make this switch
05 CONTENT Main teaching
Part 1 — Why I walked away from high-ticket
  • The clients I kept attracting were total beginners
  • They needed foundational work — tech, content, sales, confidence — before they could even use what I was giving them
  • And I just wasn't comfortable giving them more than they were able to handle
  • When they didn't renew, I took it personally. I had poured everything into them.
  • But the truth is — they weren't in a position to receive it
  • Here's how I think about it through Kabbalah:
    • If you meet someone you love and in two weeks you give them a Rolex — that's not giving. That's taking.
    • Because you're giving to a vessel that can't hold it
    • Doing the right thing at the wrong time is still the wrong thing
  • And I realized — that's exactly what I was doing with high-ticket clients who weren't ready
  • A lot of people will just sell to anyone with a credit card and a pulse. That's not right. And I refuse to do it.
  • The other thing — I was drained. DMs, ghosted discovery calls, cameras off, last-minute cancels. I just couldn't bring myself to do it anymore.
  • As a 4/6 Projector, my success is literally contingent on the success I bring to my clients. I take that seriously. So when I tried running free weekly calls with people who weren't invested — they didn't take action, they complained, and I just didn't enjoy it. That's not why I'm here.
✦ Visual Pause — The Vessel Metaphor
🪣
Client Not Ready to Receive
The gift is too much, too soon. It spills over. Nothing sticks. You gave everything — but the vessel couldn't hold it.
🍵
Right Client, Right Time
The cup fits perfectly. They're ready. Your work lands. They transform — and they remember who helped them do it.
"Doing the right thing at the wrong time
is still the wrong thing."
Part 2 — The fear of switching
  • What I was most afraid of? People thinking I wasn't valuable anymore.
  • "Oh, she must have been failing — why else would she charge less?"
  • I was scared people wouldn't respect me. Value me. I thought it made me look like a failure.
  • I had to get over that. Because the model I was running was not the right one for where I was going.
✦ Key Rebuttal Moment
"You're Always Spending Something"
A math reframe — not a judgment of the path
⏰ Time-Rich Path
Go live on TikTok daily
Get active in other Skool groups
Build organically through content
Longer timeline · Lower risk
💸 Money-Rich Path
Run paid ads
Test offers with ad budget
Buy data and speed
Faster timeline · Higher risk
The question was never which model makes more money.
It's: what's your time horizon — and what can you actually sustain?
Part 3 — The real math of $8/month
  • Think about it this way:
    • 1,000 people × $8/month = $8,000/month minimum
    • That's the same as one $8K coaching package
    • But with the package, one client leaving can break you
    • With a membership, your income is not volatile. It's scalable.
  • And $8 is easy to say yes to. That's the whole point.
  • A membership is an inherently unattractive offer — people don't want more subscriptions
  • So you make the entry so easy they'd be crazy to say no
✦ Model Comparison — Low-Ticket vs High-Ticket
Same destination. Very different roads.
$8/Month Community
$8K High-Ticket
Barrier to join
Low — easy yes
High — requires trust + sales process
Sales infrastructure
Minimal
Calls, funnels, DMs, personal brand
Income stability
Distributed across many
Vulnerable to one client leaving
Client commitment
Self-directed
High-touch, high expectation
Scales with
Volume + community network effect
Conversion skill + personal brand strength
Time to first dollar
Lower barrier
Higher barrier
The higher the price point, the more sophisticated your sales, marketing, and personal brand need to be — these are assets that take time to build.
✦ Math Reframe — Same Destination, Different Roads
Same Destination, Different Roads
A math reframe — not a judgment of the model
The Membership Path
$8,000
1 client × $8,000
= $8,000/month

One relationship. One renewal. One point of failure.
VS
The Churn Reality
≈ $8,000
400 members × $20/month
= $8,000/month

Lose 10% monthly = 40 members gone. Replace them just to stay flat.
🏃
At 10% monthly churn on 400 members, you must replace 40 people every single month just to stay even. That's not growth — that's a treadmill. → → → going nowhere.
Note: This is about understanding the math of churn — not a verdict on which model is better.
  • My model right now:
    • $8/month — entry, access to core resources
    • $88/month — Premium, includes weekly group coaching
    • $444/year VIP — turned on when I have availability, private sessions + done-for-you
  • I still do high-ticket — but it's by invitation only now. A very select few who I know I can genuinely help and who are in a position to receive it.
  • It's just not my main offer anymore. And honestly? That shift changed everything.
  • I love working with high-capacity, spiritually inclined women who are about that life. And I've learned that even a small amount of investment completely changes how people show up — they take action, they get results, and that's what I'm here for.

Part 4 — Why Skool specifically
  • Skool replaces your email list, landing page, funnel, website, payment processor, course platform, community, calendar, CRM — in one place
  • Once someone is on Skool and has a credit card in, they're more likely to join another community — including yours
  • Skool will literally send you free members from other communities with similar interests. No other platform does that.
  • Built-in Facebook pixel integration — easy to target Skoolers with ads
  • The more engaged someone is in your community, the more results they get, the longer they stay, and the higher your customer lifetime value
  • Also — with ads? A book-a-call funnel costs $200–$300 per booked call. An $8/month offer is a completely different game.

Built-in Facebook Pixel integration — retarget your community members with ads easily
Dashboard analytics — MRR, channel sources, profile visits, conversion rates, all in one view
Members from other communities are sent to you automatically based on shared interests
The more engaged your members, the longer they stay — and the higher your lifetime value per member

The bar to get your first $8 member is so much lower than your first $8,000 client.
And that momentum — that first yes — is what builds the confidence and the data to scale everything else.
Part 5 — What this model is actually for (and who it's not for)
  • This model IS for you if:
    • You value community and want to serve people at scale
    • You're willing to build in public and co-create with your members
    • You don't want to be the only resource — you want to create systems
    • You're not comfortable with high-ticket yet, or you've been burned by it
  • This model is NOT for you if:
    • You only want to serve one person at a time deeply — and that fills you up
    • You don't have any audience AND don't know how to run ads — it won't pay your bills overnight
    • You're not willing to test, pivot, and build as you go
  • Both models are perfectly okay. It has to come from you — otherwise you're copy-pasting someone else's strategy.
  • The question I'd ask yourself: "What would your higher self do?" How do you want your day to actually look?
✦ Is This for You? Be Honest With Yourself.
Low-Ticket Community — Is It Your Path?
✦ This IS for you if...
You want to build alongside your community and co-create your offer
You're comfortable spending time OR money to grow — TikTok lives, being active in groups, or running ads
You want income that isn't dependent on any single client
You're okay with a longer time horizon in exchange for more stability and scale
You want multiple entry points into your world at different price levels
You value nervous system safety over high-risk, high-reward
✦ This is NOT for you if...
You want the fastest possible path to $20K months
You're not yet comfortable building a personal brand in public
You're not willing to test and validate your offer through ads, going live, or being active in other communities
You need immediate high income and can't sustain a longer runway
You prefer deep, focused work with one person at a time over serving many
These aren't verdicts. They're requirements of each path — knowing which one fits is the whole point.
Part 6 — What I know now that I wish I'd known
  • Start with your best idea and build alongside your members — don't build a full course library before you launch
  • You can never outperform or out-optimize a bad offer. You don't know what a good offer is until you test.
  • High-ticket first was the right move for me — that's how I learned what my market actually needed
  • I wouldn't have known what to build without it. But I couldn't stay there forever.
  • Sometimes things aren't working and it doesn't mean you're doing anything wrong
  • Ask yourself: What is this trying to teach me? Not why is this happening to me?
06 CLOSE + CTA Warm wrap-up

There is no one right way. Base it on what you actually want — because then there's always a way to get there.

🎨 THUMBNAIL PROMPTS — NANO BANANA Upload your headshot to Nano Banana, then paste the prompt below
Thumbnail 1 — Transformation
The "Before & After" Visual
"With the uploaded photo of the creator as the subject, place her center-frame with a slightly surprised-but-knowing expression — raised brow, subtle smirk, confident. The background is split vertically: left half deep navy with a large gold '$8,000' fading out, right half soft lavender-to-purple gradient with a bright '$8' glowing in neon lime green. Dramatic side lighting, left side slightly shadowed, right side lit warm. Text overlay top-right in bold white Montserrat: 'WHY I QUIT'. 1280×720px, high contrast."
Why this works: The price contrast is viscerally confusing — the brain has to resolve it, which forces the click.
Thumbnail 2 — Moment
The "Walking Away" Energy
"With the uploaded photo of the creator as the subject, show her with a bold, unbothered expression — slight smirk, chin up, completely unbothered. Background: deep royal purple with a single large glowing disco ball reflection overhead, subtle holographic shimmer behind her. Clean negative space to her right. Text overlay in large bold white Montserrat on the left: 'LEFT $8K' with a neon lime line break, then below in smaller text: 'on purpose'. 1280×720px."
Why this works: The phrase "on purpose" is the disruptor — it reframes the story from failure to intentional choice, triggering curiosity.
Thumbnail 3 — Novelty
The "$8 Reveal"
"With the uploaded photo of the creator as the subject, she is pointing directly at camera with a huge, slightly chaotic grin — like she's about to tell you the most obvious secret in the world. Background is hot pink with bold white text LARGE on the right: '$8/MO' with a small emoji-style arrow pointing to it. Lighting: bright, flat, high energy. Optional: very light holographic shimmer overlay. The energy is playful and confident, not salesy. 1280×720px."
Why this works: The absurdly low price next to a confident, energetic expression creates dissonance — viewers need to know what the catch is.
Thumbnail 4 — Story
The "Real Talk" Close-Up
"With the uploaded photo of the creator as the subject, ultra-close crop — just her face, slight lean forward like she's about to tell you something nobody else will say. Expression: direct eye contact, slight intensity, a hint of 'I've been waiting to say this.' Background: deep dark navy-purple, almost black, with a very subtle lavender glow behind her head. Minimalist. Text overlay bottom-left in Montserrat bold white, two lines: 'THE TRUTH' and below in smaller weight: 'about memberships'. 1280×720px."
Why this works: Close-crop eye contact creates intimacy and perceived exclusivity — feels like insider information, not a YouTube video.
